trades.sentiment_summary: Recent news highlights Alkermes (ALKS) receiving a 'Moderate Buy' consensus from brokerages with a $44.69 price target, alongside institutional buying like Allianz Asset Management acquiring 511,702 shares[1][2]. Insider sales occurred on February 2, 2026, including EVP Craig Hopkinson selling 9,000 shares under a 10b5-1 plan and Director Shane Cooke selling 61,200 shares via Rule 144[1][3][4]. No major operational news emerged in the last 24 hours, with upcoming earnings on February 10 noted[5].

Astera Labs announced the opening of a new research and development design center in Israel on February 9, 2026, focusing on AI scale-up fabrics, high-bandwidth connectivity, and addressing memory bottlenecks in AI training and inference[3]. The stock rose 18.93% amid this news, outperforming peers, with elevated trading volume 27% above the 20-day average[3]. Allianz Asset Management purchased a new stake in the company[4].

Wheaton Precious Metals stock opened up 4.0% on Monday with institutional buying activity from multiple funds. However, analyst sentiment is mixed, with a consensus 'Buy' rating tempered by valuation concerns, as the stock trades at a P/E ratio of 61.7x—nearly double peer averages—suggesting significant upside may already be priced in.

Arista Networks (ANET) shows bullish momentum with pre-market trading at $137.23 and opening up 6.9% at $137.50, supported by institutional buying from Profund Advisors ($7.10M position) and Large Capital Growth Fund (9,142 shares).[3][4][6] Analyst consensus remains 'Moderate Buy' with an average price target of $164.44, highlighting AI revenue growth to $2.75B in 2026 and strong positioning in AI infrastructure.[1][2][4] Recent analyses note high P/E concerns but emphasize upside potential to $184 by 2027 despite a 16% drop from highs.[1][2]

Alamos Gold Inc. (AGI) opened at $40.19 on February 9, 2026, with reports of a 6.7% increase, continuing positive momentum amid strong analyst support including 'Buy' ratings and price targets up to $60 from Scotiabank.[5] Forecasts predict slight upward movement to around $40.2, backed by 70% bullish technical indicators, while institutional adjustments like a position cut by Ashton Thomas were noted but overshadowed by broader optimism.[2][5] Analyst consensus remains 'Buy' with an average target of $45.50.[5]

Recent news highlights positive momentum in ASX 200 financials, with banks like ANZ, Westpac, and QBE gaining, alongside broker buy recommendations for ANZ and upgrades for lithium miner PLS[1][2]. Insider buying at First Au (ASX:FAU) signals confidence in the gold explorer, while AU Small Finance Bank saw a slight price gain amid investor interest[3][5]. Tech and mining sectors faltered last week, but upbeat bank earnings outlooks and selective buy calls provide mild optimism[1][2].

ARKK shares traded between $66.30 and $70.58 on February 9, 2026, closing at $70.55 with elevated volume of 11.26M shares versus an average of 8.9M. Stock analysis sites report mixed short-term signals but have upgraded ARKK to a buy candidate due to positive trends and short-term buying opportunities. No major negative news events were reported in the last 24 hours.

Goldman Sachs raised its price target for KE Holdings (BEKE) from $18.60 to $19 on February 8, 2026, while downgrading the rating from Buy to Neutral due to the need for further visibility into property market stabilization.[1] Despite the downgrade, overall analyst consensus remains strongly bullish with a median 1-year price target of $21.02 implying 14% upside, and no Sell ratings among covering analysts.[1] Institutional activity shows mixed signals, including a large share sale by C WorldWide Group, but ongoing buybacks from November 2025 to January 2026 highlight active capital management.[2][6]

U.S. stocks opened slightly lower on February 9, 2026, with the S&P 500 slipping 0.2% and the Dow down 0.1% in early trading after Friday's strong rally[1][4]. Broader market news highlights positive earnings momentum, especially in tech driving 61% of EPS growth, alongside mentions of stocks like Palantir as top performers[1][3]. No specific news on AS stock (likely Amer Sports) appears in the last 24 hours[1][2][3][4].

No significant news articles specifically about Berkshire Hathaway Class B (BRK.B) stock were published in the last 24 hours as of February 9, 2026, 4 PM UTC. Available data shows recent trading activity with shares around $501-$509 on February 8, and Morningstar noting it trades at a premium to its $965 fair value estimate from October 2025. Options trading for Barrick Mining (B) was mentioned but is unrelated to BRK.B.

Axalta Coating Systems (AXTA) faces upcoming earnings on February 10, expected to show flat EPS on lower revenues amid sequential declines, with consensus estimates revised lower recently.[1][3] Coverage highlights a pending merger with AkzoNobel targeting mid-2026 shareholder vote and year-end close, promising $600M synergies, though it prompted some analyst downgrades to Hold in January.[3][5] Recent stock performance shows short-term gains contrasting longer-term declines, with analysts maintaining a neutral-to-Buy consensus and fair value suggesting undervaluation.[2][4]

BILL Holdings reported strong Q2 2026 results, beating revenue and EPS estimates with $414.7M revenue and $0.64 EPS, leading to a 37% stock surge post-earnings. Analysts maintain a 'Moderate Buy' consensus with an average price target around $61, supported by raised FY26 guidance and positive notes on profitability and growth. Some price target cuts and minor insider selling provide slight counterbalance.

No specific news articles about Century Aluminum (CENX) from the last 24 hours (Feb 8-9, 2026) were identified in the search results. The most recent items include a Feb 6 report of CENX hitting a new 52-week high of $52.78 and analyst consensus ratings as of Feb 8 showing a 'Buy' recommendation[1][6]. Earlier developments, such as the Hawesville site sale and new smelter plans on Feb 2 and Jan 26, have been positively received with stock gains[3][5][7].
